projects.title: PET Filament Recycling Machine
projects.tagline: Turning plastic waste into 3D-printer filament — the award-winning CODET build.
projects.description: A machine that recycles PET plastic waste (like used bottles) into usable 3D-printer filament. Built and presented at the CODET Engineering Competition (Council of Deans of Engineering & Technology), South-East zone, where it helped secure 1st and 3rd place overall and a place at the national finals in Abuja. It tackles plastic pollution while producing a low-cost material for makers and engineers.
highlights: Led the team that presented the machine at the CODET South-East competition
Took 1st & 3rd place overall and qualified for the national finals in Abuja
Converts waste PET into 3D-printing filament — sustainability meets engineering

projects.title: Quadruped Robot Dog
projects.tagline: A four-legged walking robot with servo-driven, gait-controlled legs.
projects.description: A four-legged walking robot built from the ground up. Each leg is servo-driven, and a custom gait controller coordinates the legs so the robot balances and walks on its own across surfaces.
highlights: Servo-driven legs with coordinated gait control
Balances across each step for stable walking
Built and programmed end-to-end with Arduino

projects.title: Quadruped Spider
projects.tagline: A multi-leg spider walker with synchronised servo locomotion.
projects.description: A spider-style walking robot that drives many servos in synchrony to produce a smooth, insect-like crawling motion. A study in multi-limb coordination and balance.
highlights: Coordinates many servos for smooth crawling

projects.title: Self-Driving Car
projects.tagline: An autonomous car prototype that senses and steers itself.
projects.description: An autonomous car prototype that senses its surroundings and steers itself using onboard control logic — no hands on the wheel. Combines sensing, decision logic, and motor control.
highlights: Sensor data handling and control logic in Python

projects.title: Obstacle-Avoiding Car
projects.tagline: Ultrasonic sensing with real-time auto steer-around.
projects.description: A robot car that fires ultrasonic pulses to detect obstacles ahead, then autonomously steers around them in real time. A clean demonstration of sense-think-act robotics.

projects.title: Smart Home System
projects.tagline: Home automation for lights, devices & security — on command.
projects.description: A home-automation system that controls lights, appliances, and security remotely through connected ESP microcontrollers. Brings everyday devices online and under control.
highlights: Remote control of lights, appliances & security

projects.title: Wearable Health Monitor
projects.tagline: A wearable that tracks vital signs in real time.
projects.description: A wearable device that tracks vital health signals like heart rate in real time and reports them back to the wearer — engineering meets well-being.

projects.title: Smart Door Lock
projects.description: An electronic smart lock built around an ESP microcontroller, providing secure, controlled access to a door from a connected device.

projects.title: AI Voice Calculator
projects.tagline: A voice-controlled calculator powered by speech AI.
projects.description: A voice-controlled calculator that recognises spoken numbers and operations using speech AI, then returns the result hands-free. A friendly intro to applied AI and voice interfaces.
highlights: Recognises spoken input via speech AI

awards.title: 1st & 3rd Place Overall
awards.desc: Helped lead the team to first and third place overall at the regional CODET (Council of Deans of Engineering & Technology) Engineering Competition, presenting a PET plastic filament recycling machine — qualifying for the national finals in Abuja.

achievements.title: CODET National Finalist
achievements.desc: Qualified to represent the South-East zone at the national CODET Engineering Competition in Abuja.

achievements.title: GDG & RAIN Community Lead
achievements.desc: Currently leading two campus tech communities — Google Developer Groups and RAIN — driving events and engagement.
